title: Contributing to zztable1
text: non-trivial changes, please open issue first discuss approach. blueprint rendering pipeline several extension points brief design discussion saves rework.

title: Contributing to zztable1
text: Fork repository create topic branch main. Install development dependencies. zztable1 uses renv; restore pinned environment : Make changes. Keep commits focused; prefer many small commits one large one. Add update tests tests/testthat/. New exported functions require tests. Include cases output format (console, HTML, LaTeX) adding rendering features. Run devtools::document() regenerate man/ NAMESPACE. Run full check locally: check must pass errors, warnings, notes standard ‘New submission’ note. Update NEWS.md one-line bullet unreleased section. Open pull request main. Reference related issues.
code: renv::restore() devtools::check()

title: Contributing to zztable1
text: Use native R pipe (|>); avoid %>% new code. Use <- assignment, never =. Use snake_case functions variables. Prefer implicit returns; reserve return() early exits. Document exported functions roxygen2. must @title, @description, @param, @return, @examples. Two-space indentation. Single quotes character literals. add ‘’ comments. Reserve comments non-obvious ‘’.

title: Patch Plan: zztable1 Size-Header Misalignment Bug
text: Observed ADCS MCI report (arm character vector appearance order Placebo, Vitamin E, Donepezil): rendered Table 1 header showed Placebo (N=253), Vitamin E (N=259), Donepezil (N=257), whereas true sizes Placebo=259, Vitamin E=257, Donepezil=253.
code: library(zztable1) d <- data.frame(   arm = c(rep("Placebo", 259), rep("Vitamin E", 257), rep("Donepezil", 253)),   age = rnorm(769, 73, 7) ) bp <- table1(arm ~ age, data = d, size = TRUE) bp$metadata$dimensions$group_info$sizes #>   Placebo Vitamin E Donepezil #>       253       259       257     <- WRONG: Placebo is 259, not 253 table(d$arm) #> Donepezil   Placebo Vitamin E #>       253       259       257     <- table() is alphabetical

title: Customizing Statistics and Tests in zztable1
text: ’ll use simulated clinical trial dataset demonstrate customization options: Sample Clinical Trial Dataset
code: # Create comprehensive clinical trial dataset set.seed(123) n_per_group <- 40  clinical_data <- data.frame(   # Treatment groups   treatment = factor(rep(c("Placebo", "Low Dose", "High Dose"), each = n_per_group)),      # Patient characteristics   age = c(     rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 65, sd = 12),    # Placebo     rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 63, sd = 10),    # Low Dose       rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 67, sd = 15)     # High Dose   ),      # Primary efficacy endpoint (with treatment effect)   efficacy_score = c(     rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 20, sd = 8),     # Placebo: lower scores     rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 28, sd = 9),     # Low Dose: moderate improvement     rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 35, sd = 7)      # High Dose: best improvement   ),      # Safety endpoint (non-normal distribution)   biomarker_level = c(     rexp(n_per_group, rate = 0.1),             # Placebo: exponential distribution     rexp(n_per_group, rate = 0.08),            # Low Dose     rexp(n_per_group, rate = 0.12)             # High Dose   ),      # Binary outcomes   response = factor(c(     sample(c("Responder", "Non-responder"), n_per_group, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.3, 0.7)),     sample(c("Responder", "Non-responder"), n_per_group, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.6, 0.4)),     sample(c("Responder", "Non-responder"), n_per_group, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.8, 0.2))   )),      # Categorical safety outcome   safety_grade = factor(c(     sample(c("None", "Mild", "Moderate", "Severe"), n_per_group, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.4, 0.4, 0.15, 0.05)),     sample(c("None", "Mild", "Moderate", "Severe"), n_per_group, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.3, 0.45, 0.2, 0.05)),     sample(c("None", "Mild", "Moderate", "Severe"), n_per_group, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.2, 0.5, 0.25, 0.05))   ), levels = c("None", "Mild", "Moderate", "Severe")) )  # Display sample data knitr::kable(head(clinical_data),               caption = "Sample of Clinical Trial Dataset")

title: Customizing Statistics and Tests in zztable1
text: can create custom summary functions specialized requirements:
code: # Example 1: Bootstrap confidence intervals bootstrap_ci_summary <- function(x) {   if (all(is.na(x))) return("N/A")      # Bootstrap 95% CI for mean   set.seed(42)  # For reproducibility in vignette   n_boot <- 1000   boot_means <- replicate(n_boot, {     sample_data <- sample(x[!is.na(x)], replace = TRUE)     mean(sample_data)   })      mean_est <- round(mean(x, na.rm = TRUE), 1)   ci_lower <- round(quantile(boot_means, 0.025), 1)   ci_upper <- round(quantile(boot_means, 0.975), 1)      paste0(mean_est, " (", ci_lower, "-", ci_upper, ")") }  # Example 2: Robust statistics (median with MAD) robust_summary <- function(x) {   if (all(is.na(x))) return("N/A")      med <- round(median(x, na.rm = TRUE), 1)   mad_val <- round(mad(x, na.rm = TRUE), 1)      paste0(med, " [+/-", mad_val, "]") }  # Example 3: Multi-line detailed summary detailed_summary <- function(x) {   if (all(is.na(x))) return("N/A")      mean_val <- round(mean(x, na.rm = TRUE), 1)   median_val <- round(median(x, na.rm = TRUE), 1)   sd_val <- round(sd(x, na.rm = TRUE), 1)      paste0(mean_val, " +/- ", sd_val, "\n", "(median: ", median_val, ")") }  cat("### Custom Summary: Bootstrap 95% CI\n")

title: Customizing Statistics and Tests in zztable1
text: package supports multiple statistical tests appropriate different data distributions study designs: Continuous Variable Tests: ttest: Linear model t-test (default, robust multiple groups) anova: Traditional ANOVA F-test welch: Welch’s t-test (unequal variances, two groups ) kruskal: Kruskal-Wallis test (non-parametric) Categorical Variable Tests: fisher: Fisher’s exact test (default, conservative) chisq: Chi-square test (requires adequate cell counts)
code:
cat("**Continuous Variable Tests:**

") cat("- `anova`: Traditional ANOVA F-test

") cat("- `kruskal`: Kruskal-Wallis test (non-parametric)

") cat("**Categorical Variable Tests:**
") cat("- `fisher`: Fisher's exact test (default, conservative)

title: Customizing Statistics and Tests in zztable1
text: bioequivalence studies, might prefer confidence intervals specific tests:
code: # Simulate crossover bioequivalence data set.seed(789) be_data <- data.frame(   formulation = factor(rep(c("Reference", "Test"), each = 24)),   # Primary PK parameters   cmax = c(     rnorm(24, mean = 100, sd = 20),    # Reference     rnorm(24, mean = 105, sd = 18)     # Test (slight difference)   ),   auc = c(     rnorm(24, mean = 500, sd = 80),    # Reference       rnorm(24, mean = 495, sd = 75)     # Test   ),   tmax = c(     rexp(24, rate = 0.5) + 1,          # Reference (non-normal)     rexp(24, rate = 0.6) + 1           # Test   ) )  # Custom summary for bioequivalence (geometric mean +/- %CV) geometric_mean_summary <- function(x) {   if (all(is.na(x))) return("N/A")      # Remove zeros and negative values for log transformation   x_pos <- x[x > 0 & !is.na(x)]   if (length(x_pos) == 0) return("N/A")      geom_mean <- round(exp(mean(log(x_pos))), 1)   cv_percent <- round(100 * sqrt(exp(sd(log(x_pos))^2) - 1), 1)      paste0(geom_mean, " (", cv_percent, "%CV)") }  be_footnotes <- list(   variables = list(     cmax = "Maximum plasma concentration (ng/mL)",     auc = "Area under concentration-time curve (ng*h/mL)",     tmax = "Time to maximum concentration (hours)"   ),   general = c(     "Randomized crossover bioequivalence study",     "Geometric mean and %CV shown for PK parameters",     "Non-parametric tests used for tmax (non-normal distribution)"   ) )  cat("### Bioequivalence Study Analysis\n")

code: # Simulate multi-center data set.seed(101112) center_data <- data.frame(   center = factor(paste("Center", rep(1:4, each = 30))),   treatment = factor(rep(rep(c("Active", "Control"), each = 15), 4)),   # Primary endpoint with center effects   primary_endpoint = c(     # Center 1     rnorm(15, mean = 75, sd = 12), rnorm(15, mean = 65, sd = 15),     # Center 2       rnorm(15, mean = 78, sd = 10), rnorm(15, mean = 68, sd = 12),     # Center 3     rnorm(15, mean = 72, sd = 14), rnorm(15, mean = 62, sd = 18),     # Center 4     rnorm(15, mean = 76, sd = 11), rnorm(15, mean = 66, sd = 13)   ),   # Secondary binary endpoint   response = factor(c(     # Center 1: Active vs Control     sample(c("Success", "Failure"), 15, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.7, 0.3)),     sample(c("Success", "Failure"), 15, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.4, 0.6)),     # Center 2     sample(c("Success", "Failure"), 15, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.75, 0.25)),     sample(c("Success", "Failure"), 15, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.35, 0.65)),     # Center 3     sample(c("Success", "Failure"), 15, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.65, 0.35)),     sample(c("Success", "Failure"), 15, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.45, 0.55)),     # Center 4     sample(c("Success", "Failure"), 15, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.8, 0.2)),     sample(c("Success", "Failure"), 15, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.3, 0.7))   )) )  multicenter_footnotes <- list(   variables = list(     primary_endpoint = "Primary efficacy endpoint (0-100 scale)",     response = "Binary treatment response (success/failure)"   ),   general = c(     "Multi-center randomized controlled trial",     "ANOVA used to account for treatment and center effects",      "Chi-square test for categorical outcomes (adequate sample size)"   ) )  cat("### Multi-center Trial: Overall Treatment Comparison\n")

text: vignette demonstrates two gt features zztable1 currently support: hierarchical column spanners summary rows subtotals grand totals.

title: gt Features Beyond zztable1
text: Column spanners group columns shared headers. gt supports multi-level spanners – spanners contain spanners – producing hierarchical column headers.

title: gt Features Beyond zztable1
code: trial_summary <- data.frame(   variable = c("Age", "BMI", "SBP"),   placebo_mean = c(63.2, 26.8, 132.4),   placebo_sd = c(11.5, 4.2, 18.1),   treatment_mean = c(61.8, 27.1, 128.9),   treatment_sd = c(12.3, 4.8, 16.7),   p_value = c(0.403, 0.538, 0.042) )  trial_summary |>   gt(rowname_col = "variable") |>   tab_spanner(     label = "Placebo (N=53)",     columns = c(placebo_mean, placebo_sd)   ) |>   tab_spanner(     label = "Treatment (N=67)",     columns = c(treatment_mean, treatment_sd)   ) |>   cols_label(     placebo_mean = "Mean",     placebo_sd = "SD",     treatment_mean = "Mean",     treatment_sd = "SD",     p_value = "P-value"   ) |>   fmt_number(decimals = 1) |>   fmt_number(columns = p_value, decimals = 3) |>   tab_style(     style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),     locations = cells_body(       columns = p_value,       rows = p_value < 0.05     )   ) |>   tab_header(     title = "Baseline Characteristics",     subtitle = "Single-level column spanners"   )

title: gt Features Beyond zztable1
text: Spanners can contain spanners. top-level spanner ‘Vital Signs’ groups two sub-spanners, groups columns.
code: vitals <- data.frame(   visit = c("Baseline", "Week 4", "Week 8", "Week 12"),   sbp_placebo = c(132.4, 131.8, 130.2, 129.5),   dbp_placebo = c(84.1, 83.6, 82.9, 82.4),   sbp_treatment = c(128.9, 124.3, 120.1, 118.6),   dbp_treatment = c(82.7, 80.1, 78.4, 76.9),   hr_placebo = c(72.3, 71.8, 72.0, 71.5),   hr_treatment = c(73.1, 72.4, 71.8, 71.2) )  vitals |>   gt(rowname_col = "visit") |>   tab_spanner(     label = "Placebo",     columns = c(sbp_placebo, dbp_placebo),     id = "bp_placebo"   ) |>   tab_spanner(     label = "Treatment",     columns = c(sbp_treatment, dbp_treatment),     id = "bp_treatment"   ) |>   tab_spanner(     label = "Blood Pressure",     spanners = c("bp_placebo", "bp_treatment"),     id = "bp_top"   ) |>   tab_spanner(     label = "Heart Rate",     columns = c(hr_placebo, hr_treatment)   ) |>   cols_label(     sbp_placebo = "SBP",     dbp_placebo = "DBP",     sbp_treatment = "SBP",     dbp_treatment = "DBP",     hr_placebo = "Placebo",     hr_treatment = "Treatment"   ) |>   fmt_number(decimals = 1) |>   tab_stubhead(label = "Visit") |>   tab_header(     title = "Vital Signs by Visit",     subtitle = "Three-level column hierarchy: "   ) |>   tab_source_note(     "Blood Pressure > Heart Rate > Arm"   )

title: gt Features Beyond zztable1
text: column names follow delimiter convention, gt can auto-generate spanner structure.
code: labs <- data.frame(   patient = paste("Patient", 1:5),   hematology.wbc = c(6.2, 7.1, 5.8, 8.4, 6.9),   hematology.rbc = c(4.5, 4.8, 4.2, 5.1, 4.6),   hematology.platelets = c(245, 310, 198, 276, 255),   chemistry.glucose = c(98, 112, 95, 140, 103),   chemistry.creatinine = c(0.9, 1.1, 0.8, 1.4, 1.0),   chemistry.alt = c(22, 35, 18, 48, 26) )  labs |>   gt(rowname_col = "patient") |>   tab_spanner_delim(delim = ".") |>   fmt_number(     columns = c(       hematology.wbc, hematology.rbc,       chemistry.creatinine     ),     decimals = 1   ) |>   fmt_number(     columns = c(       hematology.platelets,       chemistry.glucose, chemistry.alt     ),     decimals = 0   ) |>   tab_stubhead(label = "Patient") |>   tab_header(     title = "Laboratory Results",     subtitle = "Spanners generated from column name delimiters"   )

title: gt Features Beyond zztable1
text: gt computes per-group subtotals table-wide grand totals using arbitrary aggregation functions. summary rows lazy-evaluated render time.

title: gt Features Beyond zztable1
code: enrollment <- data.frame(   site = c(     rep("Site A", 3), rep("Site B", 3), rep("Site C", 3)   ),   month = rep(c("Jan", "Feb", "Mar"), 3),   screened = c(45, 52, 38, 30, 28, 35, 22, 25, 31),   enrolled = c(32, 41, 28, 22, 20, 27, 15, 18, 24),   excluded = c(13, 11, 10, 8, 8, 8, 7, 7, 7) )  enrollment |>   gt(     rowname_col = "month",     groupname_col = "site"   ) |>   summary_rows(     fns = list(       Total = ~ sum(.),       Mean = ~ round(mean(.), 1)     ),     side = "bottom"   ) |>   tab_stubhead(label = "Month") |>   tab_header(     title = "Enrollment by Site",     subtitle = "Per-group totals and means"   ) |>   tab_style(     style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),     locations = cells_summary()   )

title: gt Features Beyond zztable1
code: enrollment |>   gt(     rowname_col = "month",     groupname_col = "site"   ) |>   summary_rows(     fns = list(`Site Total` = ~ sum(.)),     side = "bottom"   ) |>   grand_summary_rows(     fns = list(`Grand Total` = ~ sum(.)),     side = "bottom"   ) |>   tab_header(     title = "Enrollment by Site",     subtitle = "Subtotals and grand total"   ) |>   tab_style(     style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),     locations = cells_summary()   ) |>   tab_style(     style = list(       cell_text(weight = "bold"),       cell_fill(color = "#e8e8e8")     ),     locations = cells_grand_summary()   )

title: gt Features Beyond zztable1
text: Summary rows accept function. compute median alongside sum.
code: ae_data <- data.frame(   system = c(     rep("Gastrointestinal", 4),     rep("Neurological", 3),     rep("Dermatological", 3)   ),   event = c(     "Nausea", "Vomiting", "Diarrhea", "Abdominal pain",     "Headache", "Dizziness", "Insomnia",     "Rash", "Pruritus", "Alopecia"   ),   placebo_n = c(12, 5, 8, 3, 15, 7, 4, 6, 3, 1),   treatment_n = c(18, 9, 14, 6, 12, 5, 3, 11, 7, 2) )  ae_data |>   gt(     rowname_col = "event",     groupname_col = "system"   ) |>   cols_label(     placebo_n = "Placebo",     treatment_n = "Treatment"   ) |>   summary_rows(     fns = list(       Total = ~ sum(.),       Median = ~ median(.)     ),     side = "bottom"   ) |>   grand_summary_rows(     fns = list(`All Events` = ~ sum(.)),     side = "bottom"   ) |>   tab_spanner(     label = "Number of Events",     columns = c(placebo_n, treatment_n)   ) |>   tab_header(     title = "Adverse Events by System Organ Class",     subtitle = "Per-group summaries with custom aggregation"   ) |>   tab_style(     style = list(       cell_text(weight = "bold"),       cell_fill(color = "#d4e8d4")     ),     locations = cells_grand_summary()   )

title: gt Features Beyond zztable1
text: examples illustrate two structural capabilities zztable1’s flat blueprint currently support: Column spanners require tree structure column headers rather flat character vector. Implementing require changes blueprint$col_names header rendering logic three output formats. Summary rows require ability insert computed rows group boundaries, aggregation functions operate group’s data. extend current row model beyond variable-header level rows. features added zztable1’s blueprint architecture. Alternatively, bridge function converts evaluated zztable1 blueprint gt-compatible data frame give users access gt’s full rendering capabilities without reimplementing .

title: NEJM Table 1 Reproduction
text: vignette reproduces visual style published NEJM Table 1 (demographic baseline characteristics) using gt package zztable1. target table Langhoff et al., ‘Sirolimus-Coated Balloon Angioplasty Infrainguinal Disease’ (NEJM 2025).

title: NEJM Table 1 Reproduction
text: data simulates structure approximate proportions published table: two-arm balloon angioplasty trial N=626 per group.
code: set.seed(2025) n_per_arm <- 626 n <- n_per_arm * 2  arm <- factor(   rep(c("Sirolimus-Coated Balloon", "Uncoated Balloon"),       each = n_per_arm) )  make_binary <- function(p1, p2, n1 = n_per_arm,                         n2 = n_per_arm) {   factor(c(     sample(c("Yes", "No"), n1, TRUE, c(p1, 1 - p1)),     sample(c("Yes", "No"), n2, TRUE, c(p2, 1 - p2))   ), levels = c("No", "Yes")) }  trial <- data.frame(   arm = arm,   age = c(     round(rnorm(n_per_arm, 75.5, 8.5), 1),     round(rnorm(n_per_arm, 75.0, 8.8), 1)   ),   sex = factor(c(     sample(c("Female", "Male"), n_per_arm, TRUE,            c(0.358, 0.642)),     sample(c("Female", "Male"), n_per_arm, TRUE,            c(0.343, 0.657))   ), levels = c("Female", "Male")),   bmi = c(     round(rnorm(n_per_arm, 25.2, 4.2), 1),     round(rnorm(n_per_arm, 25.7, 4.4), 1)   ),   diabetes = make_binary(0.439, 0.465),   hypertension = make_binary(0.770, 0.772),   dyslipidemia = make_binary(0.607, 0.588),   smoking = make_binary(0.588, 0.593),   renal_insufficiency = make_binary(0.335, 0.348),   cad = make_binary(0.411, 0.433),   antiplatelet = make_binary(0.725, 0.714),   anticoagulant = make_binary(0.335, 0.361),   lipid_lowering = make_binary(0.665, 0.665) )  trial$bmi[sample(which(trial$arm ==   "Sirolimus-Coated Balloon"), 4)] <- NA trial$bmi[sample(which(trial$arm ==   "Uncoated Balloon"), 11)] <- NA

code: bp <- table1(   arm ~ age + sex + bmi + diabetes +     hypertension + dyslipidemia + smoking +     renal_insufficiency + cad +     antiplatelet + anticoagulant +     lipid_lowering,   data = trial,   theme = "nejm",   pvalue = FALSE,   title = paste(     "Table 1. Demographic Characteristics",     "and Medical History of the Patients",     "at Baseline.*"   ),   block = list(     "Cardiovascular risk factors and coexisting conditions" = c(       "diabetes", "hypertension", "dyslipidemia",       "smoking", "renal_insufficiency", "cad"     ),     "Medications -- no. (%)" = c(       "antiplatelet", "anticoagulant",       "lipid_lowering"     )   ),   footnotes = list(     variables = list(       bmi = paste(         "The body-mass index is the weight in",         "kilograms divided by the square of",         "the height in meters."       ),       hypertension = paste(         "Arterial hypertension was defined as",         "a previous diagnosis documented in",         "medical records, current treatment",         "with antihypertensive medication, or",         "blood pressure of at least 140/90",         "mm Hg at baseline."       ),       smoking = paste(         "History of smoking was defined as",         "active smoking at the time of",         "enrollment (or in the previous 12",         "months) or previous smoking habits",         "more than 12 months before",         "enrollment."       ),       renal_insufficiency = paste(         "Renal insufficiency was defined as",         "a previous diagnosis of stage 3 or",         "higher chronic kidney disease."       )     ),     general = c(paste(       "Percentages may not total 100",       "owing to rounding. IQR denotes",       "interquartile range."     ))   ) )  render_bp(bp)

title: NEJM Table 1 Reproduction
text: gt required manual computation summary statistics (pre-computed data frame). zztable1 computed raw data via formula interface. gt used tab_row_group() section headers (e.g., ‘Cardiovascular risk factors’). zztable1 currently support mid-table section headers outside stratification system. gt offers finer control individual cell styling (indent depth, per-row background). zztable1 applies theme-level CSS uniformly. gt can show title bar spanning full table width. zztable1 yet render table titles.

title: Quickstart Guide: zztable1
text: zztable1 next-generation R package creating publication-quality summary tables (Table 1) flexible blueprint-based architecture. package features lazy evaluation, sparse storage, medical journal themes (NEJM, Lancet, JAMA).

title: Quickstart Guide: zztable1
code: devtools::install_github("rgt47/zztable1") library(zztable1)

title: Quickstart Guide: zztable1

code: set.seed(42) n <- 120  trial <- data.frame(   arm = factor(     sample(c("Placebo", "Treatment"), n, replace = TRUE),     levels = c("Placebo", "Treatment")   ),   age = round(rnorm(n, 62, 14)),   sex = factor(     sample(c("Male", "Female"), n, replace = TRUE)   ),   bmi = round(rnorm(n, 27, 5), 1),   diabetes = factor(     sample(c("No", "Yes"), n, replace = TRUE,            prob = c(0.7, 0.3))   ),   site = factor(     sample(paste("Site", LETTERS[1:3]), n,            replace = TRUE)   ) )  trial$bmi[sample(seq_len(n), 5)] <- NA

title: Simple Table 1 Example
text: vignette demonstrates basic usage zztable1 small simulated clinical trial dataset.
code: set.seed(123) trial_data <- data.frame(   arm = factor(rep(c("Treatment", "Placebo"), each = 50)),   age = rnorm(100, mean = 45, sd = 15),   sex = factor(     sample(c("Male", "Female"), 100, replace = TRUE)   ),   bmi = rnorm(100, mean = 26, sd = 5) )  create_table(   formula = arm ~ age + sex + bmi,   data = trial_data,   theme = "nejm",   pvalue = TRUE )

title: Column Spanners and Summary Rows
text: vignette demonstrates two structural extensions zztable1 blueprint framework: Column spanners group columns hierarchical headers Summary rows insert computed subtotals group boundaries features applied post-hoc existing blueprint using pipeline-style API.

title: Column Spanners and Summary Rows
code: set.seed(42) n <- 120  trial <- data.frame(   arm = factor(     sample(c("Placebo", "Treatment"), n, replace = TRUE),     levels = c("Placebo", "Treatment")   ),   age = round(rnorm(n, 62, 14)),   sex = factor(     sample(c("Male", "Female"), n, replace = TRUE)   ),   bmi = round(rnorm(n, 27, 5), 1),   diabetes = factor(     sample(c("No", "Yes"), n, replace = TRUE,            prob = c(0.7, 0.3))   ) )

title: Column Spanners and Summary Rows
text: single-level spanner groups treatment arm columns shared label.
code: bp <- table1(   arm ~ age + sex + bmi + diabetes,   data = trial,   pvalue = TRUE,   theme = "nejm" )  add_spanner(bp, label = "Treatment Arms",             columns = c(2L, 3L))  render_bp(bp)

title: Column Spanners and Summary Rows
text: Two separate spanners, covering different column ranges.
code: bp2 <- table1(   arm ~ age + sex + bmi + diabetes,   data = trial,   pvalue = TRUE,   totals = TRUE,   theme = "lancet" )  add_spanner(bp2, label = "By Arm",             columns = c(2L, 3L), id = "arms") add_spanner(bp2, label = "Overall",             columns = 4L, id = "overall")  render_bp(bp2, "lancet")

title: Column Spanners and Summary Rows
text: parent spanner groups two child spanners, producing two-level column hierarchy.
code: bp3 <- table1(   arm ~ age + sex + bmi + diabetes,   data = trial,   pvalue = TRUE,   totals = TRUE,   theme = "jama" )  add_spanner(bp3, label = "By Arm",             columns = c(2L, 3L), id = "by_arm") add_spanner(bp3, label = "Overall",             columns = 4L, id = "overall") add_spanner(bp3, label = "Patient Characteristics",             spanners = c("by_arm", "overall"))  render_bp(bp3, "jama")

title: Column Spanners and Summary Rows
text: Summary rows computed numeric values variable group inserted group boundary.
code: bp4 <- table1(   arm ~ age + sex + bmi,   data = trial,   pvalue = FALSE,   theme = "nejm" )  add_summary_rows(bp4,   fns = list(     "Group n" = function(x) sum(!is.na(x))   ),   side = "bottom" )  render_bp(bp4)

title: Stratified Table 1 Analysis Examples
text: Stratified analysis crucial component epidemiological clinical research. vignette demonstrates create stratified Table 1 analyses using zztable1 package. Stratified tables allow examine patterns within subgroups data, revealing important differences might obscured overall analyses.

title: Stratified Table 1 Analysis Examples
text: Let’s create comprehensive clinical trial dataset includes multiple potential stratification variables. ‘data.frame’: 300 obs. 12 variables: $ treatment : Factor w/ 3 levels “Placebo”,“Low Dose”,..: 2 2 1 2 3 3 2 1 3 2 … $ site : Factor w/ 4 levels “Site ”,“Site B”,..: 1 4 2 4 1 1 2 3 1 2 … $ sex : Factor w/ 2 levels “Female”,“Male”: 2 2 1 2 2 2 1 2 2 2 … $ age_group : Factor w/ 3 levels “18-44”,“45-64”,..: 2 2 3 2 3 1 2 2 3 2 … $ disease_severity: Factor w/ 3 levels “Mild”,“Moderate”,..: 1 1 1 2 3 3 1 1 2 1 … $ age : num 34 29 53 61 24 62 64 77 60 61 … $ bmi : num 27.5 28.3 26.5 29.1 28.3 28.8 28.6 33.5 22.1 32.8 … $ systolic_bp : num 128 132 148 126 144 145 117 134 125 143 … $ diabetes : Factor w/ 2 levels “”,“Yes”: 1 1 1 2 1 1 1 1 1 1 … $ hypertension : Factor w/ 2 levels “”,“Yes”: 1 1 1 2 1 1 1 2 1 1 … $ hemoglobin : num 13.9 12.4 13.4 12 14.6 10.1 13.5 13.2 15.1 12.8 … $ creatinine : num 0.94 1.54 1.23 0.79 0.71 1.17 1.01 1.07 1.05 1.3 … treatment site sex age_group disease_severity age bmi systolic_bp 1 Low Dose Site Male 45-64 Mild 34 27.5 128 2 Low Dose Site D Male 45-64 Mild 29 28.3 132 3 Placebo Site B Female 65+ Mild 53 26.5 148 4 Low Dose Site D Male 45-64 Moderate 61 29.1 126 5 High Dose Site Male 65+ Severe 24 28.3 144 6 High Dose Site Male 18-44 Severe 62 28.8 145 7 Low Dose Site B Female 45-64 Mild 64 28.6 117 8 Placebo Site C Male 45-64 Mild 77 33.5 134 9 High Dose Site Male 65+ Moderate 60 22.1 125 10 Low Dose Site B Male 45-64 Mild 61 32.8 143 diabetes hypertension hemoglobin creatinine 1 13.9 0.94 2 12.4 1.54 3 13.4 1.23 4 Yes Yes 12.0 0.79 5 14.6 0.71 6 10.1 1.17 7 13.5 1.01 8 Yes 13.2 1.07 9 15.1 1.05 10 12.8 1.30
code: # Create a realistic multi-center clinical trial dataset set.seed(42) n <- 300  clinical_data <- data.frame(   # Primary treatment variable   treatment = factor(     sample(c("Placebo", "Low Dose", "High Dose"), n, replace = TRUE,             prob = c(0.4, 0.3, 0.3)),     levels = c("Placebo", "Low Dose", "High Dose")   ),      # Potential stratification variables   site = factor(sample(paste("Site", LETTERS[1:4]), n, replace = TRUE)),   sex = factor(sample(c("Male", "Female"), n, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.55, 0.45))),   age_group = factor(     sample(c("18-44", "45-64", "65+"), n, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.3, 0.4, 0.3)),     levels = c("18-44", "45-64", "65+")   ),   disease_severity = factor(     sample(c("Mild", "Moderate", "Severe"), n, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.4, 0.4, 0.2)),     levels = c("Mild", "Moderate", "Severe")   ),      # Baseline characteristics   age = round(rnorm(n, 58, 15)),   bmi = round(rnorm(n, 26.5, 4.2), 1),   systolic_bp = round(rnorm(n, 135, 18)),      # Comorbidities   diabetes = factor(sample(c("No", "Yes"), n, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.75, 0.25))),   hypertension = factor(sample(c("No", "Yes"), n, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.65, 0.35))),      # Lab values   hemoglobin = round(rnorm(n, 13.2, 1.8), 1),   creatinine = round(rnorm(n, 1.1, 0.3), 2) )  # Add some realistic missing values clinical_data$bmi[sample(1:n, 8)] <- NA clinical_data$hemoglobin[sample(1:n, 5)] <- NA clinical_data$creatinine[sample(1:n, 3)] <- NA  # Show dataset structure str(clinical_data) head(clinical_data, 10)

title: Stratified Table 1 Analysis Examples
text: Understanding baseline characteristics vary across different study sites crucial multi-center trials. Key Observations: - study site shows separate table section - Within-site treatment group comparisons - Helps identify site-specific recruitment patterns - Essential assessing treatment balance across sites
code: create_table(   treatment ~ age + sex + bmi + diabetes + systolic_bp,   data = clinical_data,   strata = "site",   theme = "nejm",   pvalue = TRUE,   totals = TRUE )

title: ToothGrowth Analysis: A Complete Table 1 Example
text: Let’s use custom summary function highlight dose-response pattern:
code: # Custom summary emphasizing range and median for dose-response dose_response_summary <- function(x) {   if (all(is.na(x))) return("N/A")      med <- round(median(x, na.rm = TRUE), 1)   q1 <- round(quantile(x, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE), 1)   q3 <- round(quantile(x, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE), 1)   range_val <- round(max(x, na.rm = TRUE) - min(x, na.rm = TRUE), 1)      paste0(med, " [", q1, "-", q3, "]\n(range: ", range_val, ")") }  create_table(   formula = dose ~ len,   data = ToothGrowth,   theme = "lancet",   pvalue = TRUE,   numeric_summary = dose_response_summary,   footnotes = list(     general = "Values shown as median [IQR] with range below"   ) )

title: zztable1: Advanced Publication-Ready Summary Tables
text: zztable1 package provides next-generation architecture creating publication-ready summary tables (commonly called “Table 1”) used biomedical research clinical trials. vignette demonstrates key features capabilities package.

title: zztable1: Advanced Publication-Ready Summary Tables
text: Lazy Evaluation Architecture: Fast blueprint creation computation demand Journal-Specific Theming: NEJM, Lancet, JAMA, BMJ formatting styles Advanced Footnote System: Variable-specific, column-specific, general footnotes superscript markers Multiple Output Formats: Console, LaTeX, HTML output proper column headers Flexible Statistics: Built-custom summary statistics Stratified Analysis: Support subgroup analyses Full Compatibility: interface original zztable1 package R Markdown Integration: Automatic format detection seamless PDF/HTML output

title: zztable1: Advanced Publication-Ready Summary Tables

title: zztable1: Advanced Publication-Ready Summary Tables
text: Let’s start basic example using mtcars dataset:
code: # Prepare data data(mtcars) mtcars$transmission <- factor(   ifelse(mtcars$am == 1, "Manual", "Automatic"),   levels = c("Automatic", "Manual") ) mtcars$engine_type <- factor(   ifelse(mtcars$vs == 1, "V-shaped", "Straight"),   levels = c("Straight", "V-shaped") )  # Create basic summary table create_table(transmission ~ mpg + hp + wt, data = mtcars)

text: Include p-values group comparisons:
code: create_table(transmission ~ mpg + hp + wt,              data = mtcars,              pvalue = TRUE)

title: zztable1: Advanced Publication-Ready Summary Tables
text: Add overall summary column:
code: create_table(transmission ~ mpg + hp + wt,              data = mtcars,              pvalue = TRUE,              totals = TRUE)

title: zztable1: Advanced Publication-Ready Summary Tables
text: package provides several built-summary statistics: Mean (SD) format: Median [IQR] format: Mean +/- SE format:
code:
# Default: Mean (SD) cat("Mean (SD) format:
") create_table(transmission ~ mpg + hp, data = mtcars) # Median [IQR] cat("
Median [IQR] format:
") create_table(transmission ~ mpg + hp, data = mtcars,              numeric_summary = "median_iqr") # Mean +/- SE cat("
Mean +/- SE format:
") create_table(transmission ~ mpg + hp, data = mtcars,              numeric_summary = "mean_se")

title: zztable1: Advanced Publication-Ready Summary Tables
text: Create summary statistics: Custom Median (Min-Max) format:
code: # Custom function: Median (Min-Max) custom_summary <- function(x) {   med <- round(median(x, na.rm = TRUE), 1)   min_val <- round(min(x, na.rm = TRUE), 1)   max_val <- round(max(x, na.rm = TRUE), 1)   paste0(med, " (", min_val, "-", max_val, ")") }  cat("Custom Median (Min-Max) format:\n") create_table(transmission ~ mpg + hp, data = mtcars,              numeric_summary = custom_summary)

title: zztable1: Advanced Publication-Ready Summary Tables

code: # Create stratification variable mtcars$cylinder_group <- factor(   ifelse(mtcars$cyl <= 4, "4-cylinder",   ifelse(mtcars$cyl <= 6, "6-cylinder", "8-cylinder")),   levels = c("4-cylinder", "6-cylinder", "8-cylinder") )  # Stratified analysis create_table(transmission ~ mpg + hp,              data = mtcars,              strata = "cylinder_group",              pvalue = TRUE)

code: # Standard clinical trial baseline table create_baseline_table <- function(data, treatment_var, theme = "nejm") {   formula_str <- paste(treatment_var, "~ .")   bp <- table1(as.formula(formula_str),                 data = data,                theme = theme,                pvalue = TRUE,                footnotes = list(                  general = list(                    "Data are mean (SD) or n (%)",                    "P-values from ANOVA or chi-squared test"                  )                ))   return(bp) }  # Example usage # bp_standard <- create_baseline_table(trial_data, "treatment") cat("Utility function created for standardized baseline tables\n")

title: Create Memory-Efficient Table1 Blueprint Object — Table1Blueprint
text: optimized blueprint uses environment-based sparse storage instead pre-allocating cells. Benefits include: Memory usage scales actual content, table dimensions O(1) hash-table lookup cell access Automatic garbage collection unused cells Support large sparse tables
